correctness.notes: Conjecture nondecomp, parts (b) and (c): For the allowed law p0=p1=1/2, q_{m|0}=q_{m|1}=1/2, r_{0,m}=1/4, r_{1,m}=3/4, one has kappa(P)=1/2 but for every 0<=lambda<=1/4, I(lambda)=B_box(lambda)=B_sens(lambda)=[-lambda,lambda]. At lambda=0 this gives I(0)=B_sens(0)={0}, not a strict inclusion.; thm:vertex converse from the response-type LP back to the locked shared-U model: The proof creates one latent state per positive rho_{t,a}; a basic feasible solution of the displayed LP can have up to 6 positive rho components, while the setup locks |U|<=5. The note lacks the needed arm-averaging reconstruction, e.g. bar a^0=p0^{-1} sum_a rho_{0,a} a and bar a^1=p1^{-1} sum_a rho_{1,a} a, which would give a two-atom completion preserving the LP objective by linearity.; prop:inference-active-branch bootstrap statement: The proposition says only 'multiplier draws xi_i'; the proof requires E xi_i=0, E xi_i^2=1, finite 2+delta moment, and independence from the sample. With arbitrary multiplier draws, the bootstrap statistic t_n^{-1}{Phi(hat eta_n+t_n h)-Phi(hat eta_n)} applied to Zhat_eta^b need not estimate the limiting law.

perItemFindings.label: nondecomp
perItemFindings.one_line: [blocking] Conjecture nondecomp, parts (b) and (c): For the allowed law p0=p1=1/2, q_{m|0}=q_{m|1}=1/2, r_{0,m}=1/4, r_{1,m}=3/4, one has kappa(P)=1/2 but for every 0<=lambda<=1/4, I(lambda)=B_box(lambda)=B_sens(lambda)=[-lambda,lambda]. At lambda=0 this gives I(0)=B_sens(0)={0}, not a strict inclusion. → Strict inclusion on the whole relatively open set {kappa(P)!=0} is false, and the claimed strict compatibility-free corner is false without an added condition excluding q_{.|1}=q_{.|0}.

perItemFindings.label: vertex
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] thm:vertex converse from the response-type LP back to the locked shared-U model: The proof creates one latent state per positive rho_{t,a}; a basic feasible solution of the displayed LP can have up to 6 positive rho components, while the setup locks |U|<=5. The note lacks the needed arm-averaging reconstruction, e.g. bar a^0=p0^{-1} sum_a rho_{0,a} a and bar a^1=p1^{-1} sum_a rho_{1,a} a, which would give a two-atom completion preserving the LP objective by linearity. → As written the LP-to-model sharpness proof does not close under the stated support class; the theorem needs a corrected converse argument, not merely an invocation of the K<=5 assumption.

perItemFindings.label: inference
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] prop:inference-active-branch bootstrap statement: The proposition says only 'multiplier draws xi_i'; the proof requires E xi_i=0, E xi_i^2=1, finite 2+delta moment, and independence from the sample. With arbitrary multiplier draws, the bootstrap statistic t_n^{-1}{Phi(hat eta_n+t_n h)-Phi(hat eta_n)} applied to Zhat_eta^b need not estimate the limiting law. → Bootstrap validity needs these multiplier hypotheses in the statement or in ass:inference-regularity.

Fix: Strict inclusion on the whole relatively open set {kappa(P)!=0} is false, and the claimed strict compatibility-free corner is false without an added condition excluding q_{.|1}=q_{.|0}.
[serious, conjecture:vertex] thm:vertex converse from the response-type LP back to the locked shared-U model: The proof creates one latent state per positive rho_{t,a}; a basic feasible solution of the displayed LP can have up to 6 positive rho components, while the setup locks |U|<=5. The note lacks the needed arm-averaging reconstruction, e.g. bar a^0=p0^{-1} sum_a rho_{0,a} a and bar a^1=p1^{-1} sum_a rho_{1,a} a, which would give a two-atom completion preserving the LP objective by linearity.. Fix: As written the LP-to-model sharpness proof does not close under the stated support class; the theorem needs a corrected converse argument, not merely an invocation of the K<=5 assumption.
[serious, conjecture:inference] prop:inference-active-branch bootstrap statement: The proposition says only 'multiplier draws xi_i'; the proof requires E xi_i=0, E xi_i^2=1, finite 2+delta moment, and independence from the sample. With arbitrary multiplier draws, the bootstrap statistic t_n^{-1}{Phi(hat eta_n+t_n h)-Phi(hat eta_n)} applied to Zhat_eta^b need not estimate the limiting law.. Fix: Bootstrap validity needs these multiplier hypotheses in the statement or in ass:inference-regularity.

Referee notes: I reproduced the collapse, compactness/nesting, the nondecomposition witness, the finite response-type LP, and the endpoint-inference expansion. The core collapse/compactness and conditional inference expansions are sound, but the nondecomposition solve target is false as stated and the vertex proof has a load-bearing converse gap under the locked K<=5 support convention.

correctness.notes: Conjecture~\ref{conj:nondecomp}, parts (b) and (c): Under the allowed law \(p_0=p_1=1/2\), \(q_{m|0}=q_{m|1}=1/2\), \(r_{0,m}=1/4\), \(r_{1,m}=3/4\), the ACE reduces to \(\theta=\sum_{m,u}(1/2)d(m,u)\pi(u)\). Thus the leak bound forces all three intervals inside \([-\lambda,\lambda]\), and the displayed completion attains every \(t\in[-\lambda,\lambda]\). The note's claimed strict surviving term is absent: there is no residual shared-\(U\) tightening term when \(q_{\cdot|1}=q_{\cdot|0}\), even though \(\kappa(P)=1/2\).

perItemFindings.label: nondecomp
perItemFindings.one_line: [blocking] Conjecture~\ref{conj:nondecomp}, parts (b) and (c): Under the allowed law \(p_0=p_1=1/2\), \(q_{m|0}=q_{m|1}=1/2\), \(r_{0,m}=1/4\), \(r_{1,m}=3/4\), the ACE reduces to \(\theta=\sum_{m,u}(1/2)d(m,u)\pi(u)\). Thus the leak bound forces all three intervals inside \([-\lambda,\lambda]\), and the displayed completion attains every \(t\in[-\lambda,\lambda]\). The note's claimed strict surviving term is absent: there is no residual shared-\(U\) tightening term when \(q_{\cdot|1}=q_{\cdot|0}\), even though \(\kappa(P)=1/2\). → The strict-inclusion claim is false for every candidate \(\lambda_0(P)>0\) in this allowed regime, and the asserted corner \(I(0)\subsetneq B_{\mathrm{sens}}(0)\) is also false since all three sets equal \(\{0\}\) at \(\lambda=0\). The conjecture needs a changed regime or a weaker statement.

Referee notes: I reproduced the collapse/compactness support facts, the nondecomposition counterexample, the finite response-type LP equivalence, and the inference expansion. The vertex and inference derivations reproduce, but the stated nondecomposition conjecture is false under its own \(\kappa(P)\neq0\) regime.

reason: Case 2(2c) regime_defining theorem-split (over Case 6b stage_neg1, which fails because the proposal is salvageable: part (a), conj:vertex, conj:inference all reproduce; only conj:nondecomp (b)-(c) is false). Regression pattern (a): same nondecomp counterexample flagged in both reviews (revise then reject) — a stable, localized conjecture-level defect with a named fix, not wandering. The {κ(P)≠0} regime is the wrong characterization (counterexample has κ=1/2 yet no strict inclusion when q_{·|1}=q_{·|0}); the true regime additionally needs an active mediator channel q_{·|1}≠q_{·|0}. Over-precision test (2a') fails (this restricts WHICH laws, not an attainment/closure form), so split rather than statement_correction. loop_guard clear (theorem_splits=0); split is not collapsed (conditional strict inclusion still requires the shared-U LP/compatibility argument).
proposed_action: Theorem-split conj:nondecomp on the active-mediator regime: (1) Demote the strict-inclusion claim — Conjecture (non-decomposition) parts (b) and (c) (lines 144-151, label conj:nondecomp) — to an open Conjecture in the open-questions block (relabel only; keep its statement verbatim, including the {P:κ(P)≠0} regime and the I(0)⊊B_sens(0) corner). Part (a) (the ⊆ inclusion for all λ≥0) is unaffected and remains a main claim. (2) Add assumption A-mediator-shift verbatim to the §6 assumption block (alongside ass:leak, ~line 349); introduce NO other unstated premise. (3) Restate the §7 Main Theorem conditional on ass:leak ∧ A-mediator-shift: on {P:κ(P)≠0 ∧ q_{·|1}≠q_{·|0}} there is λ_0(P)>0 such that for 0<λ<λ_0(P) the inclusion [L(λ),U(λ)]⊆B_box(λ)∩B_sens(λ) is strict in both factors, with the corner facts of former (c). (4) List the unconditional characterization — "exact observable regime in which strict non-decomposition holds once A-mediator-shift is removed (i.e. the role of κ(P) at q_{·|1}=q_{·|0})" — as a remaining open Conjecture in the open-questions block, citing the q_{m|0}=q_{m|1}=1/2, r_{0,m}=1/4, r_{1,m}=3/4 witness.
cite: conj:nondecomp parts (b)-(c) (lines 145-151) vs eq:kappa; referee witness p0=p1=1/2, q_{m|0}=q_{m|1}=1/2, r_{0,m}=1/4, r_{1,m}=3/4 gives κ=1/2 but I(λ)=B_box(λ)=B_sens(λ)=[-λ,λ]

proposed_assumption.label: A-mediator-shift
proposed_assumption.statement: Active mediator channel (front-door non-degeneracy): the treatment shifts the mediator law, i.e. q_{·|1} ≠ q_{·|0} (equivalently ∃ m with q_{m|1} ≠ q_{m|0}). All strict-inclusion / non-decomposition claims (Conjecture non-decomposition parts (b)-(c)) are made on the sub-regime {P : κ(P) ≠ 0 and q_{·|1} ≠ q_{·|0}}; the ⊆ inclusion of part (a) is unconditional.
proposed_assumption.source: Standard front-door non-degeneracy: when q_{·|1}=q_{·|0} the M→Y mediator channel carries no treatment variation, so the shared-U structure cannot tighten beyond the box/sensitivity bounds — it is the regime kernel the conjecture must condition on, not the headline ID result, and it leaves the collapse/interval/vertex/inference results untouched.

assumption_classifications.label: A-mediator-shift
assumption_classifications.one_line: Part (b) IS the characterization claim 'strict non-decomposition on {κ(P)≠0}'; requiring an active mediator channel converts 'characterize the regime' into 'given an active mediator channel, strict inclusion holds', and is exactly the condition whose absence (q_{·|1}=q_{·|0}) supplies the counterexample.

report_summary: angle=0 v4 verdict=ACCEPT tier=field S=0 N=0 C=0

correctness.notes: Theorem 2 / \eqref{eq:vlp}: The displayed finite response-type LP \eqref{eq:vlp} is missing. The reproduced LP is: optimize over rho_{t,a}>=0 the objective sum_{a in A_lambda}(rho_{0,a}+rho_{1,a}) ell_a(P), where ell_a(P)=sum_m{q_{m|1}a_{m,1}-q_{m|0}a_{m,0}}, subject to sum_a rho_{0,a}=p_0, sum_a rho_{1,a}=p_1, sum_a rho_{0,a}a_{m,0}=p_0 r_{0,m}, and sum_a rho_{1,a}a_{m,1}=p_1 r_{1,m} for m=0,1. The note references this LP and its basis matrices, but does not state these objective and constraint terms.

perItemFindings.label: vertex
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Theorem 2 / \eqref{eq:vlp}: The displayed finite response-type LP \eqref{eq:vlp} is missing. The reproduced LP is: optimize over rho_{t,a}>=0 the objective sum_{a in A_lambda}(rho_{0,a}+rho_{1,a}) ell_a(P), where ell_a(P)=sum_m{q_{m|1}a_{m,1}-q_{m|0}a_{m,0}}, subject to sum_a rho_{0,a}=p_0, sum_a rho_{1,a}=p_1, sum_a rho_{0,a}a_{m,0}=p_0 r_{0,m}, and sum_a rho_{1,a}a_{m,1}=p_1 r_{1,m} for m=0,1. The note references this LP and its basis matrices, but does not state these objective and constraint terms. → The endpoint formula U(lambda;P)=max_B c_B^T A_B^{-1}b and L(lambda;P)=min_B c_B^T A_B^{-1}b is not mathematically defined from the text as written; adding the missing LP display and ell_a definition should repair the theorem without changing the derived claim.

The reproduced LP is: optimize over rho_{t,a}>=0 the objective sum_{a in A_lambda}(rho_{0,a}+rho_{1,a}) ell_a(P), where ell_a(P)=sum_m{q_{m|1}a_{m,1}-q_{m|0}a_{m,0}}, subject to sum_a rho_{0,a}=p_0, sum_a rho_{1,a}=p_1, sum_a rho_{0,a}a_{m,0}=p_0 r_{0,m}, and sum_a rho_{1,a}a_{m,1}=p_1 r_{1,m} for m=0,1. Fix: The endpoint formula U(lambda;P)=max_B c_B^T A_B^{-1}b and L(lambda;P)=min_B c_B^T A_B^{-1}b is not mathematically defined from the text as written; adding the missing LP display and ell_a definition should repair the theorem without changing the derived claim.

Referee notes: I reproduced the collapse, nesting, counterexample, finite response-type reduction, converse construction, and fixed-lambda inference expansion. The algebraic derivations check out, but the load-bearing finite LP referenced as \eqref{eq:vlp} is absent from the note, so Theorem 2's endpoint formula is not actually stated as written.

correctness.notes: Proposition 3 / kink non-Gaussian clause: The reproduced kink limit is (min_{j∈A_L^*} ∇v_j^L(η,λ^*)^T Z_η, max_{j∈A_U^*} ∇v_j^U(η,λ^*)^T Z_η). The note adds that this law is non-Gaussian whenever active branches have distinct gradients, but the missing condition is variance in the induced linear-form differences: for two gradients a,b one needs (a-b)^T Σ_η (a-b)>0. Since Σ_η is singular on the cell-probability simplex, distinct ambient gradients can differ only in a covariance-null direction, e.g. proportional to (1,1,1,1,0,0,0,0), giving (a-b)^T Z_η=0 a.s.; Theorem 2 / first maximizing-cell threshold rule: The reproduced endpoint LP gives finite rational basis values, but the note's threshold rule assumes the cleared equalities v_B^U(P,λ)=v_{B'}^U(P,λ) have a finite positive root set. In degenerate cases two bases can have identical value functions or tied active values over an interval, so the equality polynomial is identically zero and there is no smallest positive feasible root without quotienting identical branches or imposing a tie-breaking/genericity convention.

perItemFindings.label: inference
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Proposition 3 / kink non-Gaussian clause: The reproduced kink limit is (min_{j∈A_L^*} ∇v_j^L(η,λ^*)^T Z_η, max_{j∈A_U^*} ∇v_j^U(η,λ^*)^T Z_η). The note adds that this law is non-Gaussian whenever active branches have distinct gradients, but the missing condition is variance in the induced linear-form differences: for two gradients a,b one needs (a-b)^T Σ_η (a-b)>0. Since Σ_η is singular on the cell-probability simplex, distinct ambient gradients can differ only in a covariance-null direction, e.g. proportional to (1,1,1,1,0,0,0,0), giving (a-b)^T Z_η=0 a.s. → The stated sufficient condition for non-Gaussian kink behavior is false; the limit formula and coverage proof survive, but the theorem needs the stronger induced-gradient condition or must weaken the non-Gaussian claim.

perItemFindings.label: vertex
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Theorem 2 / first maximizing-cell threshold rule: The reproduced endpoint LP gives finite rational basis values, but the note's threshold rule assumes the cleared equalities v_B^U(P,λ)=v_{B'}^U(P,λ) have a finite positive root set. In degenerate cases two bases can have identical value functions or tied active values over an interval, so the equality polynomial is identically zero and there is no smallest positive feasible root without quotienting identical branches or imposing a tie-breaking/genericity convention. → The endpoint formula remains valid, but the claimed closed-form λ^* rule is not well-defined in tied-basis regimes; the theorem needs a degeneracy convention or a genericity hypothesis.

Referee notes: I reproduced the exclusion collapse, compactness/monotonicity, the nondecomposition counterexample, the response-type LP reduction, and the active-branch inference limit. The main identification/rate term lists match, but two theorem-level degeneracy claims are overstrong and need added tie/covariance qualifications.

correctness.notes: Theorem 2 / \label{thm:vertex} / finite response-type LP \eqref{eq:vlp}: The theorem and proof repeatedly invoke the finite response-type LP \eqref{eq:vlp}, but no equation labelled eq:vlp is present. The reproduced LP should have variables rho_{t,a}>=0 with constraints sum_a rho_{0,a}=p_0, sum_a rho_{1,a}=p_1, sum_a rho_{0,a}a_{m,0}=p_0r_{0,m}, sum_a rho_{1,a}a_{m,1}=p_1r_{1,m}, and objective sum_{t,a}rho_{t,a} ell_a(P), ell_a(P)=sum_m(q_{m|1}a_{m,1}-q_{m|0}a_{m,0}); this object is missing from the note.

perItemFindings.label: vertex
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Theorem 2 / \label{thm:vertex} / finite response-type LP \eqref{eq:vlp}: The theorem and proof repeatedly invoke the finite response-type LP \eqref{eq:vlp}, but no equation labelled eq:vlp is present. The reproduced LP should have variables rho_{t,a}>=0 with constraints sum_a rho_{0,a}=p_0, sum_a rho_{1,a}=p_1, sum_a rho_{0,a}a_{m,0}=p_0r_{0,m}, sum_a rho_{1,a}a_{m,1}=p_1r_{1,m}, and objective sum_{t,a}rho_{t,a} ell_a(P), ell_a(P)=sum_m(q_{m|1}a_{m,1}-q_{m|0}a_{m,0}); this object is missing from the note. → The finite-basis theorem is not mathematically well-typed/evaluable as written, and downstream references to basis matrices A_B(lambda), b(P), c_B(P,lambda), and the inference branch maps lack their defining LP source. Adding the displayed LP definition should rescue the claim; no dropped ACE term was found in the intended reduction.

Referee notes: I reproduced the load-bearing algebra for the nondecomposition refutation, the finite response-type endpoint reduction, the active-branch inference limit, and the cross-theorem dependency from vertex geometry to inference. The mathematical term lists match, but Theorem 2's central LP object is referenced as \eqref{eq:vlp} without any displayed definition, so the solve target is not evaluable as written.
